name: Chocolate Chip Croissants
recipeInstructions:
1. In a bowl, beat the butter and 1/4 cup flour until smooth.
2. Spread mixture on wax paper (on a wet cookie sheet) in a rectangle and refrigerate.
3. Heat the milk; stir in sugar and salt to dissolve.
4. Cool the mixture to lukewarm.
5. Sprinkle water with yeast; stir to dissolve.
6. Beat in the milk mixture and 3 cups flour until smooth.
7. Turn dough onto lightly floured pastry cloth and knead until smooth.
8. Let rise, covered, in a warm place, free from drafts, until doubled – about 1 hour.
9. Refrigerate for 1/2 hour.
10. On lightly floured pastry cloth, roll the dough into a 14x14 rectangle using a stockingette-covered rolling pin.
11. Place the butter mixture on half of the dough; remove paper.
12. Fold the other half over the butter and pinch edges to seal.
13. With one fold at the right, roll from center to 20x8. From the short side, fold the dough into thirds, making 3 layers; seal edges; chill for 1 hour wrapped in foil.
14. With a fold at the left, roll to 20x8 and fold; chill for 1/2 hour.
15. Repeat chilling process overnight.
16. The next day, roll the dough again and fold twice; chill for 1/2 hour.
17. Chill an additional hour.
18. To shape, cut the dough into 4 parts.
19. On lightly floured pastry cloth, roll each part into a 12-inch circle.
20. Cut each circle into 6 wedges.
21. Sprinkle chocolate chips (leaving a 1/2-inch margin all around) on each wedge.
22. Roll up starting at the wide end; form into crescents.
23. Place point side down, 2 inches apart on brown paper on a cookie sheet.
24. Cover and let rise in a warm place, free from drafts until doubled – 1 hour.
25. Preheat oven to 425°F.
26. Brush croissants with beaten egg yolk mixture (milk) before baking.
27. Bake for 5 minutes; reduce the oven to 375°F and bake for an additional 10 minutes or until the croissants are puffed and browned.
28. Cool on a rack for 10 minutes.
